context:
``

Or if you want to preserve the hibernation file (safer — just read-only, no write):

```bash
sudo mount -t ntfs-3g -o ro /dev/sda4 /mnt/windows
```

The `-o ro` alone may still be blocked if ntfs-3g refuses a hibernated volume. `remove_hiberfile` forces it but is destructive to the Windows hiber

``

Or if you want to preserve the hibernation file (safer — just read-only, no write):

```bash
sudo mount -t ntfs-3g -o ro /dev/sda4 /mnt/windows
```

The `-o ro` alone may still be blocked if ntfs-3g refuses a hibernated volume. `remove_hiberfile` forces it but is destructive to the Windows hiber
